description_long: This is a freestanding clawfoot bathtub with a distressed, mottled red and white finish, giving it a weathered and possibly antique appearance. The tub features four rounded claw-style feet that elevate it slightly above the ground, and a visible drainage hole is present at the bottom center. On one end, there is a set of exposed plumbing fixtures, including a faucet and handles, which are mounted above the rim and extend into the tub. The surface of the bathtub is textured, with visible irregularities and a matte, roughened look, suggesting it may be made of cast iron or a similar heavy material with a painted or enameled coating. The overall shape is oval with gently sloping sides, providing a deep basin suitable for soaking. There are no visible engravings or patterns aside from the claw feet, and no manufacturer branding is apparent.
description: A weathered, red-and-white freestanding clawfoot bathtub with exposed plumbing fixtures and a rough, textured surface. The tub features a deep oval basin, claw-style feet, and a visible drainage hole.
description_view_0: Front view showing the deep oval basin, two claw feet, and the sloped backrest; plumbing fixtures are visible on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the exposed plumbing fixtures, all four claw feet, and the elongated oval shape of the tub.
description_view_2: Rear view with the sloped backrest, two claw feet, and partial visibility of the plumbing fixtures on the left.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the plumbing fixtures more centrally, all four claw feet, and the tub's full length.
